title: Psychotria sangalkamensis
intro: Psychotria sangalkamensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the sangalkamensis plant. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Psychotria sangalkamensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Psychotria sangalkamensis include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used to treat digestive problems and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Psychotria sangalkamensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Psychotria, which contains over 1,800 species of plants. The plant is native to the tropical forests of Madagascar. The classification of Psychotria sangalkamensis includes its kingdom as Plantae and its order as Gentianales. Further classification places it in the family Rubiaceae, genus Psychotria, and species P. sangalkamensis.

Its leaves are reported to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which may help in managing certain health conditions. Studies have also suggested that the plant may possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it a potential candidate for treating infections.
